requirements.text: WHEN dot-notation sibling markdown documents share the same directory and base filename, the system shall present them as one logical document view with one tab per variant.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Scope; Acceptance Criteria Functional 1

requirements.text: WHEN a user opens a variant markdown file from the document tree, the system shall open the grouped document view with that variant tab active.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Success Conditions; Acceptance Criteria Functional 2-3

requirements.text: The system shall keep document tree entries visible as their original separate physical files while grouped viewing is active.
requirements.sourceRef: CR User Value; Acceptance Criteria Functional 4

requirements.text: WHEN a markdown document has no dot-notation variants, the system shall open it as a standalone document without filename tabs.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Naming Rules; Acceptance Criteria Functional 6

requirements.text: WHEN a root markdown document and dot-notation variants coexist for the same base filename, the system shall keep the root content and every variant content reachable from the logical document view.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Acceptance Criteria Functional 7; Edge Cases

requirements.text: WHEN a variant filename contains multiple dots after the base filename, the system shall preserve the remaining dot-delimited suffix as the tab key and keep that content reachable.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Naming Rules; Acceptance Criteria Functional 8

requirements.text: WHEN users switch between filename tabs, the system shall render the active tab content and metadata for the selected physical markdown file.
requirements.sourceRef: Assessment: Document content and metadata surface

requirements.text: The system shall parse dot-notation document variants using the first dot as the split between logical base and variant key, preserving all later dot segments in the variant key.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Naming Rules; MDT-138 Namespace Rules

requirements.text: The system shall sort filename tabs alphanumerically within each logical document group.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Constraints; Acceptance Criteria Functional 5

requirements.text: The system shall not rename, move, merge, hide, or create files on disk as part of document filename grouping.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Constraints; Non-Goals

requirements.text: The system shall keep non-markdown file handling unchanged by document filename grouping.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Scope Out of Scope

requirements.text: The system shall align document filename namespace parsing with MDT-138 ticket subdocument parsing semantics where both use dot-notation grouping.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Constraints; Related Ticket MDT-138; Assessment MDT-138 namespace reuse

requirements.text: The system shall constrain grouped tab content requests to resolved markdown files inside configured document paths.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Known Constraints; Security carryover

requirements.text: The system shall avoid visible delay when opening the documents view, with grouping resolution completing before or during initial document selection without blocking tree display.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Non-Functional; Assessment Dependency and Tooling Pressure

requirements.text: The system shall render filename tabs using existing project tab UI conventions.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Non-Functional; Assessment Target Feature Needs

requirements.text: IF only one dot-notation variant exists for a logical document, THEN the system shall keep that variant directly openable and render it as the active grouped variant.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Edge Cases

requirements.text: IF files have similar prefixes but different base filenames, THEN the system shall not group them unless their computed logical base and directory match exactly.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Edge Cases

requirements.text: IF the grouped active file is removed or disappears during refresh, THEN the system shall keep the documents view stable by selecting an available file in the same group or clearing the selection when none remain.
requirements.sourceRef: Assessment Verification Gaps: SSE refresh/deletion behavior

requirements.text: IF variant labels include numeric-looking values, THEN filename tab ordering shall remain predictable for user navigation, including labels such as one, two, and 10.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Edge Cases: Numeric labels sort naturally enough for expected user navigation

requirements.text: The system shall leave document tree discovery, ordering, and display unchanged while adding grouped document viewer tabs.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Constraints: Must not affect document tree discovery, ordering, or display

requirements.text: The system shall keep existing document URL and query behavior stable and refer to the active physical markdown file when grouped tabs are used.
requirements.sourceRef: Assessment Verification Gaps: document URL/query handling from MDT-150

requirements.text: The system shall keep recent document shortcuts stable and refer to the active physical markdown file when grouped tabs are used.
requirements.sourceRef: Assessment Verification Gaps: recent document shortcuts

requirements.text: The system shall keep selected tree highlighting stable and refer to the active physical markdown file when grouped tabs are used.
requirements.sourceRef: Assessment Verification Gaps: selected tree highlighting

requirements.text: WHEN document files in a grouped filename-tab set are added, changed, or removed through native document SSE updates, the system shall refresh the filename tabs from the physical document tree while keeping the active physical markdown file stable when it still exists.
requirements.sourceRef: CR:MDT-169

scenarios.title: Grouped variants show as filename tabs
scenarios.given: a document directory contains markdown files named some-name.one.md and some-name.two.md
scenarios.when: I open either file in the Documents view
scenarios.then: I see one logical some-name document view with tabs for one and two

scenarios.title: Opening a tree variant selects its tab
scenarios.given: the document tree lists some-name.one.md and some-name.two.md as separate files
scenarios.when: I select some-name.two.md from the document tree
scenarios.then: the grouped some-name document view opens with the two tab active

scenarios.title: Document tree keeps physical file entries
scenarios.given: dot-notation markdown variants are grouped in the document viewer
scenarios.when: I look at the document tree
scenarios.then: I still see each original physical file as its own tree entry

scenarios.title: Ungrouped document opens without filename tabs
scenarios.given: a markdown document has no dot-notation sibling variants
scenarios.when: I open that document from the document tree
scenarios.then: the document opens as a standalone document without filename tabs

scenarios.title: Root and variant content remain reachable
scenarios.given: a root document some-name.md exists alongside some-name.one.md and some-name.two.md
scenarios.when: I open the logical some-name document view
scenarios.then: I can reach the root content and each variant content from the grouped view

scenarios.title: Multi-dot variant key is preserved
scenarios.given: a markdown file named some-name.alpha.beta.md exists as a variant of some-name
scenarios.when: I open that file in the Documents view
scenarios.then: I see a tab keyed as alpha.beta and can read that file's content

scenarios.title: Switching tabs updates active document content
scenarios.given: a grouped document view has tabs for one and two and the one tab is active
scenarios.then: the viewer renders the content and document metadata for some-name.two.md

scenarios.title: Document SSE updates filename tabs
scenarios.given: a grouped document view is open and native document SSE is connected
scenarios.when: a sibling markdown variant is added, changed, or removed on disk and a document-change event is received
scenarios.then: the document tree remains physical, the filename tabs refresh from the updated tree, and the active physical markdown file remains selected if it still exists

obligations.title: Use one neutral filename namespace parser for document and ticket dot-notation grouping
Keep selectedFile as the active physical markdown path across tree opens, tab switches, URLs, recents, and highlighting
Render filename tabs with existing project tab UI conventions and sorted variant labels
Fetch content only for the active resolved markdown file and keep grouping local to the loaded tree
Derive grouped document filename tabs from the loaded physical tree, including main root tabs and lone variants, without mutating tree entries or files
Use native document SSE events to refresh the physical document tree and recompute filename tabs like ticket subdocument tab reconciliation
Reuse the shared RelativeTimestamp component and floating timestamp positioning for document metadata to match ticket view

testPlans.title: Shared filename namespace parser preserves first-dot variant semantics and numeric-aware sorting
Ticket subdocument namespace adapter stays aligned with shared filename parsing
Documents filename tab resolver groups markdown variants without changing physical files
Document content endpoint rejects markdown files outside configured document paths
Documents view filename tabs preserve tree files, native document SSE tab refresh, and active physical document navigation
Document filename tab component renders project tab UI, shared metadata presentation remains consistent, and selects physical file paths
Document metadata timestamp uses shared floating presentation classes

tasks.title: Extract neutral filename namespace parser and adapt ticket subdocuments
Build Documents-view filename tab resolver model
Create missing filename-tab module shells and verify runners
Lock content safety and browser acceptance for filename tabs
Integrate filename tabs with active physical document navigation
